title: OptiOps SRE instruction model — assignment metrics
disclaimer: Placeholder example. After training, run ml/scripts/evaluate_baselines.py to overwrite with measured scores.

lowest_rougeL_cases.reference: 1) Verify Redis cluster node health and failover events. 2) Check connection pool sizing vs Redis maxclients.
lowest_rougeL_cases.prediction: You should check the logs and restart the service if needed.
lowest_rougeL_cases.instruction: We see repeated 503s from payment-api after deploy. Logs show connection reset to Redis.

error_analysis.hallucination_risk: Small instruct models may invent resource sizes or regions not in the prompt. Mitigations: ground answers with RAG (Chroma KB in ml/scripts/seed_chroma.py), constrain outputs to given context, and keep humans in the loop for production changes.
failure_modes: Over-generic checklists when the prompt omits observability signals.
Omitting INR/cost angle unless the prompt or engineered system text asks for it.
Under-specified rollback criteria for stateful deploy questions.

real_world_applicability: The dashboard ML Lab surfaces these metrics for demos. Domain QLoRA improves alignment with OptiOps incident language; SQLite logs experiment rows; Chroma backs optional RAG over the incident knowledge file — matching how a real internal copilot would be shipped and regression-tested.
